attempt.detail: Erdős 387 (Schinzel) is OPEN; I did not solve it. Rigorous contribution: a clean reduction. PROVEN (Legendre/Kummer): for 1≤k≤n/2 every prime in (n−k,n] divides C(n,k), so whenever that window contains a prime, C(n,k) has a divisor >n/2 — giving c=1/2 unconditionally for all but the small-k-in-a-prime-free-window regime, which is the residual open core. Strong exact-computational evidence that the conjecture is TRUE with c=3/4 (global worst ratio D(n,k)/n is exactly 3/4, attained uniquely at n=4,k=2; per-n worst →1; no decay for fixed k). The famous n=99215,k=15 example has a divisor at 0.978n, so it does not threaten 387.

caveats: A signed banked attempt. Trust is earned via its verifier_attachments + the gate, never from claimed_status.
